turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Hello, uh, I have an Arogya Sanjeevani policy. I'm not sure if you'll know it, it's a government standard health insurance.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Yes, we're familiar with the Arogya Sanjeevani policy. Sunrise Multispeciality Clinic accepts it for both OPD and IPD services. It covers up to ₹5 lakh per family on a floater basis.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Okay, and can I use it for a planned cataract surgery?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Cataract surgery is covered under Arogya Sanjeevani. The policy has a sub-limit for cataract — up to ₹40,000 per eye per year. You'll need a pre-authorization from the insurer before the procedure.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: Who initiates the pre-auth? Me or the hospital?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Typically the hospital initiates it on your behalf once your admission date is set. Our TPA team will handle the paperwork — you just need to provide your policy number and insurer's details when you register.

turns.speaker: caller
turns.text: That's a big relief. Thank you for explaining clearly.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Happy to help! Looking forward to seeing you at Sunrise.
